questions.question_text: Explain the concept of object-oriented programming and its main principles.
questions.answer: Object-oriented programming organizes code into reusable objects that combine data and behavior. The main principles are encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between a class and an object?
questions.answer: A class is a blueprint or template that defines the structure and behavior. An object is an instance of a class created at runtime.

questions.question_text: Explain the concept of object-oriented programming and its main principles.
What is the difference between a class and an object?
questions.answer: A class defines the structure and an object is created from it.
